danger_signals.visual_form: Wild creatures and hostile entities on screen
danger_signals.meaning: Direct collision with enemies causes immediate life loss; no health meter provides buffer

danger_signals.visual_form: Level timer countdown visible during gameplay
danger_signals.meaning: Running out of time before level completion results in life loss; creates urgency
Primary antagonist leading creatures to cause chaos in New York City; visual distinction via color inversion
Mass hysteria threat; creatures are environmental hazards preventing progress

safety_signals.visual_form: Large scrolls found in level environments
safety_signals.meaning: Grant magic points for screen-clearing special attacks or rare power-ups; acquisition provides tactical advantage
Accumulating points provides life buffer; rewards player skill progression

safety_signals.visual_form: Pizza referenced in plot and minigames (Pizza Crusher level)
safety_signals.meaning: Motivational goal; represents success state and quest completion
Player tools for offense and environmental interaction; enables progression through levels

faction_indicators.color_identity: Red (canonical mascot color, though game sprites vary)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mr. Green (doppelgänger) uses green coloring as visual opposition
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: New York City urban setting; pizza-themed minigames; Domino's Pizza branding throughout

faction_indicators.color_identity: Green (inverted color scheme from The Noid)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Leadership of wild creatures; associated with chaos and mass hysteria
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Green doppelgänger design explicitly contrasts with The Noid's typical appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Causes chaos throughout New York City; linked to creature attacks across all levels

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Diverse enemy designs per level; stage-specific hostiles
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Varies by stage; stage 13 shows alternate Noid sprite designs suggesting early iteration use
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Distributed throughout urban and specialized level environments

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: New York City map screen displays areas under threat; Mr. Green and creatures actively causing disruption
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mayor calls upon The Noid; creatures roam freely; time pressure indicates urgency of threat

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban environments (pier, buildings, city streets) with visible enemies and obstacles; stage 13 features building-scaling with distinct sprite variants
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: The Noid must navigate while avoiding/defeating enemies; time countdown drives pacing

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Confrontation with major threats; pizza-eating contest minigames represent boss battles
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Specialized challenge mechanics replace standard platforming; elevated difficulty signals boss state

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Pizza reward visualized in ending; Domino's Pizza branding validates success
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Chaos resolved; creatures defeated; New York City threat eliminated

environmental_storytelling.example: New York City setting explicitly tied to The Noid defending metropolitan area; localization changed from Japanese island theme park to establish American urban context
Mr. Green's green coloring serves as visual inversion of The Noid's canonical red, immediately communicating enemy status without dialogue
Stage 1 presents pier environment; stage 13 features building-scaling challenge; each locale tells story through platforming demands
Stage 13 Noid sprites differ in facial features from rest-of-game sprites, suggesting early-build asset repurposing and story-within-development
Pizza Crusher minigame ties mechanical challenge directly to Domino's Pizza brand integration and quest reward structure
Game opens with news report of Mr. Green and creature chaos, establishing world threat through media narrative device

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Movement across platforms; weapon usage via yo-yo to clear scrolls and interact with environment
Screen-clearing special attacks available after magic point accumulation; enhanced offensive capability
Skateboard traversal and autogyro flight represent altered movement mechanics; stage-specific behavioral variation
Positioned throughout levels as environmental obstacles; patrol or stationary patterns
Direct collision triggers immediate life loss; no damage tolerance indicates lethal threat
Directing wild creatures to cause chaos; boss-level confrontation in specialized challenge scenario
Initiates quest through news bulletin; offers pizza reward as motivation; communicates world state crisis

notes: Yo! Noid is a localized advergame based on Kamen no Ninja: Hanamaru, retaining identical level design but completely reskinning narrative, protagonist, and visual themes for Domino's Pizza promotion. Visual communication strategy relies heavily on: (1) color inversion for moral opposition (green vs. red); (2) location-specific environmental storytelling (urban vs. island); (3) sprite variant tells (stage 13 anomalies); (4) integrated minigame theming (Pizza Crusher as boss encounter representation). The game communicates danger through collision mechanics (no health meter) and time pressure rather than visual health indicators. World state progression is marked through stage transitions and minigame contexts rather than dynamic environmental changes. The crisis narrative (Mr. Green's chaos) frames gameplay as essential public service, with pizza reward serving as both mechanical goal and brand integration.
sources_used: tcrf - The Cutting Room Floor (Yo! Noid article)
